repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: when a terminal process exits, "[Process Exited]" text is added to the buffer contents.

Solution: add `exitcode` field in `nvim_get_chan_info` to show the exit code in the statusline.

Related to #14986

---

I have not updated tests yet, because I wanted feedback on whether this approach is correct as its not what was proposed in [this comment](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/14986#issuecomment-1676347598).

Initially I went with first proposal, but I noticed that `nvim_list_chans` actually returns the `channel` when the buffer displays `[Process Exited]` (docs say it returns all open channels, so the channel is not yet destroyed). 

So ig we can have the stl display the exit code if there is an error without the need of a ringbfr. If I'm not missing something, this should work.

comment_data.body:
oldtest fail because of this ~~but i can't figure out why~~

I dont think this should be in a separate submodule `vim.term` but idk which other module should provide this
This is not something we need to expose in a public API. We have `vim._core/util.lua` for internal, special-purpose stuff which doesn't yet have a fully-formed "story".
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+local M = {}
+
+--- Returns the exit code string for the current buffer, given:
+--- - Channel is attached to the current buffer
+--- - Current buffer is a terminal buffer
+---
+--- @return string
+function M.get_code()

comment_data.body:
Let's call this `exitcode` since we will likely (future) want `status` as a more general concept for all kinds of channels.

```suggestion
    PUT_C(info, "exitcode", INTEGER_OBJ(chan->exit_status));
```

Let's also use virtual text to show the old/existing message? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/32e0d05d53f452a445afad161f6dda31ac054484/src/nvim/drawscreen.c#L1450-L1459

- avoids test churn
- avoids modifying buffer contents
- users with custom statuslines will be less disrupted

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -207,4 +210,18 @@ function M.setup_child_nvim(args, opts)
   return M.setup_screen(opts.extra_rows, argv, opts.cols, env)
 end
 
+--- Asserts that the exit code of chan eventually matches the expected exit code
+---
+--- @param code integer expected exit code
+--- @param chan? integer channel id, defaults to current buffer's channel
+function M.expect_code(code, chan)

comment_data.body: Thats a good idea, although having virtual text at cursor position will be an issue when window is resized as pointed out in [this comment](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37873#issuecomment-3903840035). ~~So should we show this in the bottom left as well? But that again will need some changes in the tests.~~ Can use extmarks to show the msg

```suggestion
    -- Wait for process to exit.
    expect_exitcode(0)
    -- Process has exited but channel has't been released.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -82,9 +82,9 @@ describe('terminal channel is closed and later released if', function()
   it('opened by jobstart(…,{term=true}), exited, and deleted by pressing a key', function()
     command([[let id = jobstart('echo',{'term':v:true})]])
     local chans = eval('len(nvim_list_chans())')
-    -- Wait for process to exit.
-    screen:expect({ any = '%[Process exited 0%]' })
-    -- Process has exited but channel has't been released.
+    -- wait for process to exit
+    expect_exitcode(0)
+    -- process has exited but channel has't been released

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
    -- Wait for process to exit.
    expect_exitcode(0)
    -- Process has exited but channel hasn't been released.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -101,9 +101,9 @@ describe('terminal channel is closed and later released if', function()
   it('opened by jobstart(…,{term=true}), exited, and deleted by :bdelete', function()
     command([[let id = jobstart('echo', {'term':v:true})]])
     local chans = eval('len(nvim_list_chans())')
-    -- Wait for process to exit.
-    screen:expect({ any = '%[Process exited 0%]' })
-    -- Process has exited but channel hasn't been released.
+    -- wait for process to exit
+    expect_exitcode(0)
+    -- process has exited but channel hasn't been released

comment_regular_data.body:
> otherwise close the buffer

I think this is too destructive.

I thought thats what we were going for in #27195 

I looked into some comments in #15440, something like `:term ls` won't work if we do this. Maybe not a good idea to exit by default.

> > otherwise close the buffer
> 
> I think this is too destructive.

I was thinking that output could be stored in message history. Eventually we will have more metadata on messages, and also eventually we will have persistent terminal output similar to 'undofile', so nothing is lost. 

However, we don't need to jump immediately to that, we can do something less disruptive for now.

> add `code` field in `nvim_get_chan_info` to show the exit code in the statusline.

I think `code` is a confusing name.
~~Is macos functionaltest failing due to changes in this pr?~~ Looks unrelated
@zeertzjq I suppose the current implementation does not have the resize problem?
Since it doesn't immediately follow the updated cursor position on resize, I think it doesn't have the problem.

Hey, This change had side effects. If the window with the terminal buffer is reused, the `[Process exited 0]` may show up _multiple times_ for that window, i.e., Also, with this change, if the user interacts with the buffer, by, e.g., going into insert mode and inserting anything, the window closes. Is this intentional? I'm asking because such interaction may happen on accident, and within some contexts (see https://github.com/igorlfs/nvim-dap-view/issues/147#issuecomment-3773610857), users might want to explore a finished job.

> If the window with the terminal buffer is reused

What kind of reuse?

> if the user interacts with the buffer, by, e.g., going into insert mode and inserting anything, the window closes.

How can the user go to Insert mode in a terminal buffer?

> What kind of reuse?

I can't come up with a minimal case _now_, the gist is that with nvim-dap, a terminal buffer may be assigned to an existing window, when starting a new (second) debugging session. Essentially, if the user does not close the window from the first session, instead of creating another window, nvim-dap just "reuses" the original one.

> How can the user go to Insert mode in a terminal buffer?

Well, clearly, I meant terminal mode, but that's beside the point :p

> Also, with this change, if the user interacts with the buffer, by, e.g., going into insert mode and inserting anything, the window closes.

Hasn't it always been like this?

> This change had side effects. If the window with the terminal buffer is reused, the `[Process exited 0]` may show up _multiple times_ for that window

Actually this problem can already be seen in the tests. The newly added `nvim_buf_clear_namespace` calls shouldn't be needed.

> Actually this problem can already be seen in the tests. The newly added nvim_buf_clear_namespace calls shouldn't be needed.

I see, I'll try fixing it in sometime

> Hasn't it always been like this?

Just to confirm, yes, it has always been like that. Sorry!

Thanks for the quick fix def3r!
